settings.title: BGI Nexus
settings.tagline: Beneficial general intelligence — a nexus of minds.

hero.lead: BGI Nexus is a community platform to support the development of narrow AI and beneficial AGI that serves all of humanity — through working groups, crowd-funding, and community-led action.

about.title: Where beneficial AI
about.body: BGI Nexus is where beneficial AI gets built. We connect bold ideas with the funding, expertise, and community needed to turn them into real-world impact.
about.body_soft: As a global, community-driven initiative, BGI Nexus supports the development of beneficial AI and AGI through collaborative building, transparent governance, and aligned resourcing — so that the power of AI can serve humanity, the biosphere, and future generations.

principles.body: AI in service of flourishing life — not extraction.
Transparent governance, open frameworks, shared tools.
Many value systems, many voices — held without collapse.

items.title: Working groups
items.label: open to new members
items.body: A space for members to connect expertise, share resources and ideas, and take part in focused discussions on key topics.

items.title: Sponsored projects
items.label: in active build · 2025
items.body: Community-funded teams building AI that serves humanity and the biosphere — from health to climate to language access.

items.title: Shared frameworks
items.label: frameworks · open draft
items.body: The Spiral of Flourishing and the AI Safety & Ethics framework. Open-source tools any builder can adopt.

working_groups.intro: BGI Nexus working groups bring people together to support progress towards beneficial AI. We provide a space for members to connect expertise, share resources and ideas, and take part in focused discussions on key topics.

events.intro: BGI Nexus events create opportunities for members to connect and take part in conversations that support progress towards beneficial AI.

quote.line_2: A pledge was matched with a need.
quote.side_body: This is the unit of progress on the nexus. Not papers, not roadmaps — confirmed loops. The ledger records each one, and the field hums a little louder.

projects.intro: Through our community-centred funding model, BGI Nexus supports builders creating AI, AGI, and related technologies that are open, ethical, and designed for real-world impact.

contact.title: Questions, ideas,
contact.body: Register for updates on funded projects, working group sessions, and community events — or write to us directly.

what.label: 01 · General information
what.title: What is
what.body: BGI Nexus is where beneficial AI gets built. We connect bold ideas with the funding, expertise, and community needed to turn them into real-world impact.
what.body_soft: As a global, community-driven initiative, BGI Nexus supports the development of beneficial AI and AGI through collaborative building, transparent governance, and aligned resourcing — so that the power of AI can serve humanity, the biosphere, and future generations.

principles.label: 02 · Principles
principles.title: Principles of
principles.body: At BGI Nexus, we believe AI should be developed in the service of flourishing life. That means building technologies guided by purpose, openness, human agency, fairness, and shared responsibility. Our principles encourage a shift away from extractive, short-term thinking and toward systems that are regenerative, inclusive, and designed to strengthen people, communities, and the living world.

items.body: Technologies in the service of flourishing life — not extraction. Built for what they enable, not what they extract.
Transparent governance, open frameworks, open source. The work is visible by default.
AI as a faithful ally — never a replacement for what is most valuable in human experience.
Aligned resourcing across the field. Pluralistic value systems, held without collapse.
Community-driven decisions, distributed stewardship, accountability that survives scale.

spiral.label: 03 · A living framework
spiral.title: The spiral of
spiral.body: The Spiral of Flourishing expresses our vision for human-AI symbiosis. It offers a living framework for shaping AI in ways that preserve wonder, deepen connection, strengthen resilience, and open new possibilities for meaning and creativity.
spiral.body_soft: By linking values with design, it helps guide the development of technologies that do not replace what is most valuable in human experience, but become a faithful ally to it.

cta.title: The principles only
cta.body: Working groups are the way in. Sessions are open. Bring what you bring.

hero.headline: Get involved in
hero.lead: BGI Nexus events create opportunities for members to connect and take part in conversations that support progress towards beneficial AI.

working_groups.label: ※ Working groups
working_groups.title: Where the
working_groups.body: BGI Nexus working groups bring people together to support progress towards beneficial AI. We provide a space for members to connect expertise, share resources and ideas, and take part in focused discussions on key topics.

subscribe.title: Hear when a session
subscribe.body: Calendar invites for working group sessions, virtual events, and convenings. No marketing.

hero.headline: Where
hero.lead: BGI Nexus working groups bring people together to support progress towards beneficial AI. We provide a space for members to connect expertise, share resources and ideas, and take part in focused discussions on key topics.

join.label: ※ How to join
join.title: The door is
join.body: There is no application form. Working groups are open by design — that is how we route trust across decentralised work. Read the directory, find a session, show up. Bring what you bring.

steps.title: Pick a group
steps.body: Read the directory. Pick the working group whose questions match your capacity or curiosity. There is no application.

steps.title: Show up to a session
steps.body: Sessions are open. Drop in. Listen first if you like — that is a valid contribution in itself.

steps.title: Bring what you bring
steps.body: Research, design, build, facilitation, capital, time. Pledges become attestations as the loop closes.

steps.title: Stay or leave gently
steps.body: Cadence is opt-in. Take a season off if you need to. The work goes on. The door is open.

sprints.title: Time-boxed
sprints.body: Some working groups run on a sprint cadence rather than a weekly one. Sprints are 2–5 days of focused work on a single brief — useful when a group is converging or a framework is maturing.
sprints.body_soft: The next sprint — Omni-Win Design Agent v0.3 — opens 18 May 2026.

hero.headline: What building beneficial AI
hero.lead: Our projects show what building beneficial AI looks like in practice. Through our community-centred funding model, BGI Nexus supports builders creating AI, AGI, and related technologies that are open, ethical, and designed for real-world impact.

round.label: ※ Round 01 · 2024
round.title: A compassionate
round.body: From August to December 2024, the BGI Nexus community came together to fund the first wave of beneficial AI projects. Through peer review and democratic voting, the community deployed nearly half a million dollars to support ten innovative teams.
round.body_soft: From social and environmental solutions to safer and more compassionate AI systems, each project contributes to a more democratic ecosystem of innovation that benefits people, societies, and the biosphere.

funding.title: Community-centred.
funding.body: Round 01 closed in December 2024. Future rounds will be announced through working groups and the events calendar. Builders interested in being considered for a future round can introduce themselves through the working groups that match their work.

hero.headline: Say
hero.lead: Questions, ideas, projects you're working on, working groups you want to start — we'd love to hear from you. The fastest way in is a working group session, but the inbox is open too.

registration.label: ※ Registration
registration.title: Register to join the
registration.body: A short form, no tracking. We'll add you to the calendar invites for upcoming working group sessions and write back with a personal note.

faq_intro.label: ※ Common questions
faq_intro.title: Before you
faq_intro.body: A few questions we hear most weeks. Quick answers, so the inbox is reserved for the conversations that need it.

data.title: AGI for human flourishing · weekly
data.host: Hosted by Syn Jahan + WG leads

data.title: Healthy human-AI ecosystems
data.body: A virtual gathering on how AI can support — not strain — the relationships, institutions, and ecologies it touches. Panels, working sessions, and a closing reflection.

data.title: BGI mini-hackathon
data.body: Three days of focused build time. Cross-disciplinary teams, real briefs from the working groups, light-touch facilitation. Outputs feed back into the field.

data.name: AGI for human flourishing
data.summary: Practical governance, guardrails, and conditions for beneficial AGI. The anchor working group — sets the cadence and the questions the rest of the field circles around.

data.name: Pluralistic value systems
data.summary: How does a multi-agent ecosystem hold many value systems without collapsing one onto another? Where philosophy, design, and protocol meet.

data.name: Governance & political economy
data.summary: Routing trust across decentralised contributors. Where policy meets protocol, and where the brand book lives next to the org chart.

data.name: Multi-agent alignment
data.summary: Beyond single-model alignment. Architectures, attestations, and contracts that hold a field together. The protocol layer of the work.

data.name: Offernet · resource matching
data.summary: Capacity meeting need. The resource layer of the nexus, drawing on offernet principles. Pledges, needs, and how the loops close.

data.name: Omni-Win Design Agent
data.summary: The agent framework. Released as it matures. Open to scientific and design collaborators. Sprints are time-boxed; the rest is async.

data.title: NigerGuard
data.summary: AI-powered climate resilience platform for vulnerable regions with early warning systems.

data.title: Bionexus Gaia
data.summary: Advanced AI system for biodiversity regeneration and ecosystem recovery monitoring.

data.title: AURORA
data.summary: Decentralised AI platform bringing transparency to carbon credit markets through automated verification.
data.impact: Verified $2.3M in carbon credits at 99.2% accuracy.

data.title: GlucoseDAO
data.summary: Open health platform democratising glucose data ownership while advancing diabetes research.

data.title: Sign Language Translator AI
data.summary: AI-powered sign language translation system bridging communication gaps through real-time visual recognition.
data.impact: 95% accuracy rate across 15 sign language variants.

data.title: Project Lovelock · Living Libraries
data.summary: Decentralised ecological knowledge repositories preserving indigenous wisdom and biodiversity data.

data.title: Productivity EEG-AI Agents
data.summary: Brain-signal-powered productivity system using EEG data and AI for cognitive enhancement.

data.title: Acropolis OS · Autonomous Community AGI
data.summary: Operating system using AGI for democratic decision-making in autonomous communities.

data.title: Think Inclusion Conferencing
data.summary: Inclusive design conferencing platform ensuring global events are accessible to people with disabilities.

data.title: Farmlingua
data.summary: Multilingual AI platform for smallholder farmers, breaking down language barriers in agricultural knowledge sharing.

data.name: Discord
data.summary: Asynchronous conversation across the field. Pinned channels for each working group.

data.name: Telegram
data.summary: Lower-traffic announcements. Cadence notes for the working groups.

data.name: LinkedIn
data.summary: For the cross-institutional connective tissue. Funders, partners, organisations.

data.question: Do I need a technical background to take part?
data.answer: No. Working groups need researchers, designers, facilitators, builders, and capital. Bring what you bring — listening is also a valid contribution.

data.question: Is there a next funding round?
data.answer: Round 01 closed in December 2024. Future rounds will be announced through working groups and the events calendar. The fastest way to stay close is to register here or join a working group.

data.question: Can my organisation partner with BGI Nexus?
data.answer: Yes — write to bginexus@singularitynet.io with a sentence on what you do and where you see the alignment. We respond to every email.

data.question: Can I start a new working group?
data.answer: Yes. Show up to a few existing sessions first to see how the cadence works, then propose the new group at one. The community decides.

data.question: Are sessions recorded?
data.answer: Most working group sessions are recorded with member consent and posted to the archive. Convenings are usually closed to encourage candour.
